title: The One With The Truth About London

lines.text: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is filing her nails as Ross and Ben enter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Hi Ben!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, we have a little bathroom emergency.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah go ahead.
Ben starts to go, but Ross stops him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, before we do uh, are any of Joey's special romance magazines in there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Ben) Okay! All clear!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (running to the bathroom) Thanks Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ben, its Rachel! (He closes the door.) But whatever.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Ben) Everything okay in there?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Don't talk to me now!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awww, just like his daddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey listen can you do me a big favor? The dean's office just called and said there was an urgent meeting. Can you watch Ben for like an hour?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-what about Monica?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, she isn't home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (nervous) So it would just be, me alone?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, Ben would be there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh umm...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that-y'know it's just uh, I've never done that before. Me and him alone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, he's not an ex-con.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Okay. Uh, what do I, what do I do with him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know! Just-just talk to him-entertain him and keep him alive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (Ben enters) Ben? Come here. All right, I'm gonna leave you here with Aunt Rachel for about an hour. Okay? Are you gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I think so.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) I wasn't talking to you.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: I'll be okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you soon buddy. (He hugs and kisses him.) Be back in an hour.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Bye dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye. (Ross exits.) Ahhh... (Silence) So this is fun, huh?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Uh, want something-want something to drink?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh great! How do you feel about Diet Coke?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: I'm not allowed to have soda.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Well that's pretty much all that we have-Oh! Oh! Have you ever had a virgin margarita? (Holds up a bottle of margarita mix.)

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: What's a virgin?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Water it is.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Monica and Chandler are talking. Joey and Phoebe are getting coffee.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about the second minister we meet with? I kinda liked him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean the spitter?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! It wasn't that bad!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Easy for you to say; you'll be wearing a veil.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, what about the third guy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean the guy who kept staring at your chest?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you blame him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry, I just don't like the idea of when I say, "I do," he's thinking, "Yeah, I'd do her too!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well then we still have a problem.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (returning with Joey) With what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we're trying to find someone to perform our wedding and they're all either boring or annoying or y'know, can't stop staring at the ladies. (Points to her chest.)
Joey nods his approval.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oo! You should have one of us do it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, we're getting married, married; not sixth grade married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No! It's-it's uh a real thing! Anyone can get ordained on the Internet and perform like weddings and stuff!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! A friend of mine did it and it's totally legal!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I call it!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! No! It was my idea!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys thank you very much but neither of you is marrying us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does calling it not mean anything anymore?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are going to have a legitimate member of the clergy! And when I say legitimate I mean, gay and in control of his saliva!
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Ben are sitting on the couch bored out of their minds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ben y'know when uh, when you were a baby, you and I used to hang out all the time. 'Cause I was, I was your daddy's girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: But you're not anymore!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I'm not.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: 'Cause you guys were on a break.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! We were not on a-Okay. That's fine! Fine. Y'know what Ben? One day when you are a lot older I am going to tell you that entire story over a pitcher of real margaritas, okay?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: When's my daddy coming back?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (checks her watch) Fifty-two minutes. (Pause) So no-no brothers and sisters, huh? That must be nice. You don't have to share stuff.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Sharing is good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you're one of those. But y'know what? I have two sisters of my own and we just-just tortured each other.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Really? Like how?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y'know, we would umm, repeat everything the other said, or uh, we'd jump out of closets to scare each other, or switch the sugar for the salt so they'd put salt on their cereal.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (laughs) That's a good one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah? You like that one?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Yeah, you're funny.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm funny? Oh thank God! Well hey, I've got a ton of these! Umm, oh hear-Do you want a good one? Here's a good one. Umm, you uh, you take a quarter, take a quarter and, and you blacken the edge. Right? (Does so.) And then you say to person, I bet you can't roll this quarter from your forehead to your chin without it leaving your face. And then when they do it, they're left with a big black pencil line right down the center of their face.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Can I do it to you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I-I-I-I'm funny Ben, but I'm not stupid. Okay?
Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are eating lunch as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, did you uh, find anyone to marry you guys yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, but Horny for Monica Minister called, wanting to know if we were still together.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're never gonna find anybody.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well then let me do it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joe...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no! Look, I've been thinking about it. I'm an actor right? So I won't get nervous talking in front of people.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey look it's really sweet...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no look no! I won't spit, and I won't stare at Monica's breasts! Y'know? Everyone knows I'm an ass man!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is true.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah and the most important thing is that it won't be some like, stranger up there who barely knows you. It'll be me! And I swear I'll do a really good job. Plus, y'know I love you guys and-and it would really mean a lot to me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Y'know, we haven't found anybody else.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It might be kinda cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So I can do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah you can do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right!!! Okay!! All right! Okay-okay, I gotta get started on my speech! Oh, wait a minute, Internet ministers can still have sex right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.
Phoebe enters slowly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I have a headache. A horrible headache!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Can I get you something?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, you've got to stop chattering!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here, take a couple of these. (She gets up to grab a couple of pills.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's Hexadrin.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, I don't believe in Western medicine. No, if you just apply pressure to these points right here. (She's pinching the bit of skin between her right thumb and forefinger with her left hand.) Then your hand starts to hurt and you still have a headache, so thanks. (Takes the pills.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, while we're waiting for these pills to kick in, I'm gonna sit you down on the couch. Come on. (Phoebe gets up and goes with her.) Get some nice soft pillows under your head, I'm gonna turn the TV on and you can watch whatever you want. And I'm-Sit down-(She sits down on the couch)-gonna make you some tea. And then, I'm gonna rub your feet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! My head! Oh! (He's sitting on the chair, lies back, and puts his feet up for Monica to rub.) Oh!
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is reading and there is knock on the door which she answers.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Coming.
She opens the door to reveal Ross with a pencil mark from his forehead to his chin.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have a bone to pick with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Ben learned a little trick.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Did he pull the old... (She is waving her hand up and down her face. She's thinking about the pencil mark.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right! That's right! Saran Wrap on the toilet seat so the pee goes everywhere!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah that! You know I hate practical jokes! They're mean and they're stupid and-and I don't want my son learning them!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on! Saran Wrap on the toilet seat, you don't think that's just a little funny?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was barefoot. Now tell me, the toilet thing is the only thing you taught him right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at his mark) Yes.
Central Perk, Phoebe, Chandler, and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's amazing! My headache is completely gone! What are those pills called?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hexadrin. (She gets the box out of her purse.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I love you Hexadrin! (She kisses the box.) Oh look! It comes with a story! (She pulls out the instructions and side affects paper.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Phoebe, those are like the side affects and stuff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Say what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know, the possible side affects.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! (Starts reading them.) Dizziness, nervousness, drowsiness, facial swelling, nausea, headache-Headache. Vomiting, stomach bleeding, liver damage! Now okay, I don't recall any of this coming up when you gave me these little death capsules! Oh I'm sorry, extra strength death capsules!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, relax none of that stuff ever happens! They just put it on there for legal reasons!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In case it happens.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Say hello to Reverend Joey Tribbiani! (Holds up the piece of paper bearing the proof of his ordination.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You did it! You got ordained?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I just got off the Internet! Man, there is a lot of porn out there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Our minister...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have liver damage. Ow! Oh! (She grabs the left side of her torso.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, your liver is right here. (She points to the right side of her torso.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, then I must be disoriented.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anyway, I started working on what I'm going to say for the ceremony, do you wanna hear it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now-now, listen this is just a first draft so... (Starts to read the piece of paper he brought.) "We are gathered here today on this joyous occasion to celebrate the special love that Monica and Chandler share." (Monica and Chandler like it so far.) Eh? (He continues reading.) "It is a love based on giving and receiving. As well as having and sharing. And the love that they give and have is shared and received. And through this having and giving and sharing and receiving." (Phoebe nods her approval.) "We too can share and love and have and receive."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Should we call the spitter?
Commercial Break
Carol and Susan's, there is a knock on the door and Carol opens it to reveal Rachel.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hey Rachel! (The camera cuts to her face and we see that Ben pulled the quarter trick with her as well.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: What a nice surprise! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y'know I was just in the neighborhood and I passed by your building and I thought to myself, "What's up with Carol and sweet, little Ben?"

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Can I ask what-Come on in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Umm uh, I'll make some coffee and we can uh, chat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'd love that. I would loooove... (Carol goes to make the coffee and she sits down.) So uh, so where is sweet little Ben? I would love to have a little...

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (jumping up from behind her chair) Gotcha!! (Rachel jumps up startled.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I found him! (To Ben) Very funny, come here! (She sits down on the coffee table and Ben walks up.) That is exactly why I've come here to talk to you okay?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (from the kitchen) Rach, do you want some sugar in your coffee?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes oh-(To Ben)-Do I want sugar in my coffee? (Ben nods no.) No, just some milk would be good Carol. Thanks. (To Ben) Okay, do you remember all that stuff I taught you yesterday?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (mimicking her) Remember all that stuff I taught you yesterday?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't do that.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Don't do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously, your dad doesn't like pranks.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Seriously, your dad doesn't like pranks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh damnit!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Oh damnit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Don't say that! Don't say that!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Damnit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No don't! Go back to repeating!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Damnit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh crap!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Oh crap!
Monica and Chandler's, Phoebe, Joey, Chandler, and Monica are returning from Central Perk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I feel like my face is swelling. (To Monica) Is my face swelling?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, your face is fine! Come on, none of this stuff is going to happen to you! Stop being such a baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, interesting you should call me that! Now that I may never have one! (Holds up the warning label.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay you guys, I got a little more written. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. O-okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (reading) "When I think of the love that these two givers and receivers share, I can not help but envy the lifetime ahead of having and loving and giving and..." And then I can't think of a good word for right here. (He points to the stop on the paper where he left off.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about receiving?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See Joe, not that that's not grrreat! But, one of the cool things about having somebody we know perform the ceremony is that it can be about us! Y'know, it can be more personal. You can tell stories about us!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, like the time you and I went to Atlantic City and I made you laugh so hard you threw up your whole steak?! Remember?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, not us... (Motions Joey and him.) Us! (Motions Monica and him.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I gotcha. Sorry. (To Monica) So, did you ever make him throw up a whole anything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did you ever feed him a poison capsule that made him bleed from the eyes?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It doesn't say that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Suddenly somebody knows all about the side affects!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See Joe, we want you to tell stories but y'know, romantic stories. Nice stories.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Okay. Ooh! Ooh! Okay, maybe I'll talk about London! Y'know when you two hooked up! Only, only I won't say hooked up. I'll say, "Began their beautiful journey..."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There you go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: "...by doin' it."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. All right. Umm, so uh, so how did it happen? Did your eyes meet across the room? And then the next thing y'know you're in the bathtub together and she's feeding you strawberries?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Isn't that what happened with you and the bride's maid?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!! I call that London style.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No that is not what happened with us. Well, I was umm, I was really sad that night because this guy that I was Ross's mom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And then Chandler was, was really sweet and he consoled me. And well we drank too much...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah baby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I was a perfect gentleman and I walked her to her hotel room and said goodnight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But then later that night...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah baby!
[Cut to London, Chandler's hotel room. He is getting ready for bed by doing push-ups. One push-up. Just as he gets under the covers, there's a knock on the door.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (answering the door) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (standing outside) Cute PJ's! You're really livin' it up here in London huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I was... I was exactly expecting company after...(He looks at his watch.) 9:15.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Is Joey here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, last time I saw him he was heading out the door with the bride's maid and a bucket of strawberries. So uh, you're not still upset about what that guy told ya are ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wouldn't you be?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, look it's been a really emotional time y'know, and you've had a lot to drink. And you've just got to let that go okay? I mean you were the most beautiful in the room tonight!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You kidding? You're the most beautiful woman in most rooms... (She jumps up and kisses him.) (Breaking the kiss.) Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! What's going on? You and I just made out! You and I are making out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, not anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we don't do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, I just thought it would be fun.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How drunk are you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Drunk enough to know that I want to do this. Not so drunk that you should feel guilty about taking advantage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (thinks) That's the perfect amount!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!
They run to the bed, sit down, and start making out again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (breaking the kiss) Y'know what's weird?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This doesn't feel weird!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're a really good kisser.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have kissed over four women. (They kiss again.) Do you wanna get under the covers?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hm-hmm!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!
They do so and they take off their clothes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! You are really fast!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It bodes well for me that speed impresses you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're gonna see each other naked.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you wanna do it at the same time?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Count of three?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: One!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Two!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Two!

lines.speaker: Monica and Chandler
lines.text: Three! (They lift up the covers and check each other out, then come back up with silly grins on their faces.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I think it's safe to say that our friendship is effectively ruined.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Eh, we weren't that close anyway!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh!
They start making out again, and it takes Joey trying to enter to stop them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey! Joey! Joey! J-J-Joey-Joey-J-Joey! (Monica hides under the covers as Joey enters. Remember?)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! I was just watching a movie-e-e... (Notices that the TV is turned off.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, dude I'm so sorry!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey no-no-no-no! It's cool! It's cool! I-I'll only be a second, I'm still with my bride's maid, I just-Where are those condoms you brought?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're in my bag over there. (Points.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah. (Joey walks to Chandler's bag by getting as far away from Chandler's bed as possible.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, could you leave me one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause) For just you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey listen, why don't you come downstairs with me? There's some really nice girls down there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I-I-I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, here you go buddy. (He tosses him one.) Go nuts. (Exits.)
[Cut back to Monica and Chandler telling Phoebe and Joey the story.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's what that was?! 'Cause that other thing? I thought you were on to something, but it did nothing for me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, can we change the topic? Because it's really doing nothing for me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh... (To Chandler) Can you imagine if I hadn't left you that last one? You two might've never gotten together. Ooh-ooh! Could you imagine if I sent that hooker up to the room like I was gonna?! It's like it was in the stars!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, it's totally meant to be. (To Monica) Tell him who you originally wanted to hook up with that night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Joey) What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who did you originally want to hook up with?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, fine but please don't be upset! Okay? I was really depressed okay? And really drunk! I just wanted something stupid and meaningless. I just wanted...just sex. So, when I...went to your room that night...I was actually looking...for Joey. (Joey smiles.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah baby! (Chandler glares at him.) No baby!
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, scene continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) So you came to the room looking for Joey? Did you ever in-intend on telling me about this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No because it-it didn't seem important.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it's not important? It's not important?! If it wasn't for a bride's maid you'd be marrying him (Points to Joey) not me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!! The point is that is was you that was there that night! It is you that I am marrying! It is you that I feel in love with!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And it is a love that is based on having and giving and receiv-(Shuts up on Monica's glare.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't believe it. The most romantic night of my life and I'm runner up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, please! Do you know how unbelievably glad I am that Joey was not there that night?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (Monica turns and looks at him) Now I'm a man of the cloth, but I still have feelings!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) Look there is no way you're doing this wedding now. Okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! That's not fair! It's not my fault! I was off with my bride's maid! And who's to say I would've even said yes?! (To Monica) I mean I would've said yes. Chandler look y-y-you are making way to big a deal out of this, all right? Look, everything worked out okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's just weird! Okay? I don't want to be standing their saying my vows and then having the mental image of you and Monica! I-I-I need...I don't know what I need. I need a walk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait Chandler come on, let's-it's not a big deal!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It is to me. You wanted to sleep with Batman, and instead you had to settle for Robin. (Walks out and slams the door.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! Robin is so gay!
Carol and Susan's, Rachel is talking with Ben.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So now what have we agreed?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No more pranks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And-and what else?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: That you and daddy were not on a break.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Very good.
There's a knock on the door and Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm just visiting my good friend Carol.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Your good friend?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's her last name?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Carol...Lesbian?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nice. And by the way that uh, that line down my face?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What line?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wh-wh-what line? The line that prompted a student in my last class of the day to say uh, (In a college frat boy voice) "Dude, don't you ever was your face?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I'm sorry. I'm sorry I didn't tell you but you were so mad already!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course I was mad! I told you I-I hate this stuff! Okay? It-it's not funny!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (entering from the kitchen) Hey Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: What's not funny?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Practical jokes.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Oh I...I think they're funny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You have a line down your face.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: What? (Goes and checks.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, maybe they are not funny to you...

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (yelling from the bathroom) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Or Carol! But they're funny to kids and who is it hurting?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, y'know what? I'll tell you who it hurts! It hurts the kid who has all his clothes stolen in gym class so he has to go to the principal's office wearing nothing but a catcher's mitt!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was you?! We heard about you in Junior High! Did you really just shake your fist in the air and shout, "I will be revenged?!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I will be! Listen, I don't want you teaching my son that stuff anymore. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine. Fine, but I'll have you know that once I taught him that stuff he called me Fun Aunt Rachel. And I loved being Fun Aunt Rachel but I'll go back to being Boring and Uncomfortable Aunt Rachel if that's what you want!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No that's not what I want. Uh, I'm glad you guys were bonding but I...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look he doesn't have any brothers or sisters, somebody's gonna have to teach him this stuff! And I haven't taught him anything that a normal 6-year-old doesn't know anyway!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (yelling from off camera) Crap!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I gotta go! (Runs out.)
Central Perk, Chandler is sulking on the couch as Joey enters to talk with him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey. Do you want this scone? (Holds up his plate) It came for me but it would probably rather sleep with you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, come on nothing even happened!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look Joe, I know you wanted to do the wedding...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-hey-no! If you don't want me to do it, I except that. I don't care about that. I just...I don't want you to be upset.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can I not be upset? Okay? I finally fall in love with this fantastic woman and it turns out that she wanted you first!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah for like a half an hour one night! Chandler, she wants you for the rest of her life! You're so lucky! Look what I missed out on by not being there! Although you know what? It could never have worked like you guys did, 'cause you guys are perfect for each other. Y'know, we look at you and-and we see you together and it just...it-it fits. Y'know? And you just know it's gonna last forever.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what you should say.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When you're marrying us; that's what you should say.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? I can do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd love it if you would do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But those are the words! Those exact words!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I don't know remember exactly but, it's-it's pretty much about having and giving and sharing and receiving.
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is in the kitchen as Ross and Ben are entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey I'm sorry to do this to you again but uh, is there any way you can look after Ben for a little bit? I-I've got this meeting at school. And-and he-he asked for his uh, 'Fun Aunt' Rachel, so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh! Well of course I will watch him! We have fun, don't we Ben? (He nods yes.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you later pal.
Ross turns to leave.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, okay. (Ross has a sign on his back that reads 'Poop.') Wh-Ah-ha! (Ross stops and turns.) Wait a minute. Uh Ben, I can't do it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't let him go out that way, he's got a meeting. (To Ross) You've got something here on your back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (She takes the sign off and hands it to him.) That's great. That is great. (Crumples up the paper and throws it down in anger.) What did we just finish talking about Ben?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I...

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: What did we just finish talking about Ben?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, that's it! (He runs over to Ben, but he runs past him and out the door.) Come-you-no! You are in big trouble young man!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Wait! Come on!
[Cut to the hallway, Ben runs upstairs with Ross in pursuit.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait! No! Ben, come here! I am not kidding!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No you guys... (She walks out into the hallway.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I-am-(Suddenly Ross starts screaming and comes falling down the stairs landing just in front of Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: EHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!! My God!!!!!!!!!!! Oh my God!!!!!!! (She runs over to him and finds that it was a dummy and that she had been had.)
Central Perk, Chandler and Monica are entering. Phoebe and Joey are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey-hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So are guys doing okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we talked and Monica made me see that I over reacted a little bit and some things in life are more important.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah baby! (Phoebe and Joey nod.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm really glad you guys are okay but, I just keep thinking what would happen if-if you two (Points to Monica and Joey) actually had hooked up.
Monica and Joey's, Monica is cooking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey! Dinner's ready!
Joey, whose new diet is working out great, he looks like he only weighs 375 down from 420 enters from the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Fat Joey
lines.text: What's my little chef got for me tonight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Your favorite!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ho-ho-ho, (pausing for a rest next to the fridge) fried stuff with cheese!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep! And lot's of it!

lines.speaker: Fat Joey
lines.text: Thanks sweetheart. Give me a little sugar here. (They kiss.) Okay.
Joey sits down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, in we go.
Monica gets behind him and in combination with his sliding the chair forward and her pushing with her leg manages to get up to the table.

lines.speaker: Fat Joey
lines.text: Here we go! Here we go! Here we go! (Groans and picks up a piece of food.) How you doin'?
Ending Credits
Central Perk, Joey is getting Phoebe some coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (still reading the label) Oh my God! This is a six-hour pill! (Checks her watch) That's it! I'm out of the woods! Ohh! What a relief!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good for you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's like huge weight has been lifted! 'Cause look, (reads the side affects) no hair loss, not a rash, no hives, I'm just so happy! Because no shortness of breath, no temporary euphoria-Oh.
End
